codeReview.summary: The implementation cleanly separates stored record-link decoding from caller-facing Id normalization, and the nested foreign regressions cover save/get plus cross-entrypoint hydration consistency. However, the dedicated save/read consistency regression still fails to prove the required deterministic list_limit behavior because its fixture contains only one row and relies on incidental ordering or selection rather than making the asserted row uniquely identifiable within the limited result set.

issues.description: successful_foreign_save_matches_all_read_entrypoints uses list_limit(1) and expects to find save-return-root, but the fixture only saves that single row. This does not satisfy the feature contract requirement that list/list_limit regressions make the target row uniquely identifiable instead of relying on incidental ordering. The test proves only that the sole row is returned, not that list_limit deterministically identifies the intended saved row when multiple candidates exist.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The codebase now has a two-stage hydrate normalization contract: stored foreign payloads are normalized via decode_stored_record_links, while caller-facing plain Id stripping happens later through Id deserialization or hydration. That separation was central to this feature and earlier scrutiny findings, but .factory/library does not record it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit fde1746 changes core/src/lib.rs, core/src/repository/mod.rs, and macros/src/lib.rs around decode_stored_record_links and decode_hydrated_row; C:/Users/admin/appdb/.factory/library currently only contains architecture.md, environment.md, nested-store.md, and user-testing.md.

summary: Fails scrutiny again: the code direction is sound and keeps stored RecordId decoding separate from public nested Id normalization, but the required deterministic list_limit proof is still missing because the regression fixture does not create competing rows and therefore cannot demonstrate unique identification of the target row.
